items.summary: Expand the backend hardcoded games array in backend/main.mo to at least 100 total game entries. Include a wide variety of genres (action, puzzle, arcade, racing, strategy, IO, platformer, sports, shooter, idle, etc.). Each entry must have title, description, embedUrl (publicly accessible iframe/embed URL), and thumbnailUrl fields. Retain all existing game entries and add new ones to reach the 100-game target. Use publicly accessible embed URLs from sources like CrazyGames, Poki, itch.io, or direct game hosting URLs.

Expand the backend hardcoded shows array in backend/main.mo to at least 100 total show/movie entries. Include a wide variety of genres (drama, comedy, action, thriller, sci-fi, anime, documentary, horror, romance, crime, fantasy, etc.) and a mix of TV shows and movies. Each entry must have title, description, embedUrl (YouTube trailer embed URL in the format https://www.youtube.com/embed/<videoId>), and thumbnailUrl fields. Retain all existing show entries and add new ones to reach the 100-entry target.

Update GamesSection.tsx to handle and display the expanded list of 100 games. Update the hardcoded fallback games array in GamesSection.tsx to mirror the full 100-game backend list. Ensure the responsive grid layout renders correctly with the larger dataset — consider adding a search/filter input or genre filter badges so users can browse the large catalog. The existing GamePlayer modal must remain functional for all game entries.

Update ShowsSection.tsx to handle and display the expanded list of 100 shows/movies. Update the hardcoded fallback shows array in ShowsSection.tsx to mirror the full 100-entry backend list. Ensure the responsive grid layout renders correctly with the larger dataset — consider adding a search/filter input or genre filter badges. The existing VideoPlayer modal must remain functional for all show/movie entries.
